summary: An in-memory REST service that manages Users (create and full replace) and Orders (create, read, list, delete). Users receive system-generated UUID identifiers and must have unique email addresses. Orders receive system-generated UUID identifiers, carry a restricted status vocabulary, and are accessed through thread-safe, ephemeral in-memory persistence.

groups.name: User Management
groups.summary: Capabilities for creating and fully replacing User resources with a fixed id/name/email schema, UUID generation, and email uniqueness enforced in an in-memory store.

requirements.title: Create User endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that creates a User from a supplied name and email.

acceptance.given: a creation request containing valid values for name and email
acceptance.outcome: a new User is persisted with a system-generated UUID as its id, and a success response is returned

acceptance.given: a creation request that does not include a name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a creation request that does not include an email
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes an email already held by a persisted User
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request to preserve email uniqueness

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes extra fields beyond name and email
acceptance.outcome: the persisted User contains only id, name, and email; any additional fields are discarded

requirements.title: Replace User endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that fully replaces an existing persisted User with a complete User record.

acceptance.given: an existing User and a replace request containing a complete User record including id, name, and email
acceptance.outcome: the stored User is fully replaced with the supplied record, and a success response is returned

acceptance.given: a replace request referencing an id that does not identify any persisted User
acceptance.outcome: the system indicates that the target User was not found and makes no modification

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User where the request body is missing name, email, or id
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a replace request where the id in the request body does not match the targeted User's id
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User that repeats the User's own current email
acceptance.outcome: the replacement succeeds and the User remains uniquely represented

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User that specifies an email already held by a different User
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request to preserve email uniqueness

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User that includes extra fields beyond id, name, and email
acceptance.outcome: the persisted User contains only id, name, and email; any additional fields are discarded

requirements.title: UUID identifier generation for Users
requirements.statement: The system SHALL generate a UUID for the User id upon creation and SHALL NOT allow clients to supply or modify it.

acceptance.given: a User creation request
acceptance.outcome: the id value is a valid, system-generated UUID

acceptance.given: two independent User creation requests
acceptance.outcome: each User is assigned a distinct id

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes a client-supplied id
acceptance.outcome: the system ignores the supplied id and assigns its own UUID

acceptance.given: an existing User
acceptance.outcome: the User's id remains unchanged from the value assigned at creation

requirements.title: Email uniqueness
requirements.statement: The system SHALL enforce that the email field is unique across all persisted Users for both creation and replacement operations.

acceptance.given: a User already exists with a specific email address
acceptance.when: a creation request specifies that same email
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a User already exists with a specific email address
acceptance.when: a replace request for a different User specifies that same email
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a User already exists with a specific email address
acceptance.when: a replace request for that same User repeats the current email
acceptance.outcome: the replacement succeeds and uniqueness is maintained

acceptance.given: no persisted User has a specific email address
acceptance.when: a creation or replace request specifies that email
acceptance.outcome: the operation succeeds and the new email is stored

requirements.title: User representation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL represent a User with exactly the fields id, name, and email, and SHALL NOT persist additional fields supplied by clients.

acceptance.given: any persisted User
acceptance.outcome: it contains exactly the fields id, name, and email

acceptance.given: a User has been created or replaced
acceptance.outcome: no extra computed or metadata fields are present beyond id, name, and email

requirements.title: User in-memory persistence
requirements.statement: The system SHALL persist User data solely in memory for the lifetime of the service process.

acceptance.given: a User has been persisted
acceptance.when: the running process serves subsequent requests
acceptance.outcome: the User record remains available and can be replaced within the same process instance

acceptance.given: a User has been persisted
acceptance.outcome: the previously persisted User records are no longer present

groups.name: Order Management
groups.summary: Capabilities for creating, reading, listing, and deleting Order resources with a fixed id/status/timestamp schema, system-generated UUID identifiers, a restricted status vocabulary, and thread-safe in-memory persistence.

requirements.title: Create Order endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that creates an Order from a client-provided status and timestamp, assigning a server-generated UUID as the id.

acceptance.given: a status value of "active", "cancelled", or "completed" and a provided timestamp
acceptance.outcome: the system stores an Order with a generated UUID, the given status, and the given timestamp, and returns a success response

acceptance.given: a status value outside the allowed set
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request and does not create an Order

acceptance.given: a create request that is missing a status
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a create request that is missing a timestamp
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a create request that includes extra fields beyond status and timestamp
acceptance.outcome: the persisted Order contains only id, status, and timestamp; any additional fields are discarded

requirements.title: Read Order endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that retrieves an existing Order by its UUID.

acceptance.given: an Order exists with a specific UUID
acceptance.when: a read request is submitted for that UUID
acceptance.outcome: the system returns the Order's id, status, and timestamp

acceptance.given: no Order exists for the requested UUID
acceptance.outcome: the system indicates that the Order was not found

acceptance.given: a read request with a value that is not a UUID
acceptance.outcome: the system indicates that the Order was not found

requirements.title: List Orders endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that returns a list of all stored Orders.

acceptance.given: one or more Orders exist in the store
acceptance.outcome: the system returns a collection containing every Order

acceptance.given: no Orders exist in the store
acceptance.outcome: the system returns an empty collection

requirements.title: Delete Order endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that deletes an Order by its UUID.

acceptance.given: an Order exists with a specific UUID
acceptance.when: a delete request is submitted for that UUID
acceptance.outcome: the system removes the Order from the store and returns a success response

acceptance.given: no Order exists for the requested UUID
acceptance.outcome: the system completes the request without modifying the store

acceptance.given: a delete request with a value that is not a UUID
acceptance.outcome: the system completes the request without modifying the store

requirements.title: UUID identifier generation for Orders
requirements.statement: The system SHALL generate a UUID for the Order id upon creation and SHALL NOT allow clients to supply or modify it.

acceptance.given: an Order creation request
acceptance.outcome: the id value is a valid, system-generated UUID

acceptance.given: two independent Order creation requests
acceptance.outcome: each Order is assigned a distinct id

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes a client-supplied id
acceptance.outcome: the system ignores the supplied id and assigns its own UUID

requirements.title: Order status validation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL restrict the Order status field to exactly the values "active", "cancelled", or "completed".

acceptance.given: a create request with status "active"
acceptance.outcome: the Order is created

acceptance.given: a create request with status "cancelled"
acceptance.outcome: the Order is created

acceptance.given: a create request with status "completed"
acceptance.outcome: the Order is created

acceptance.given: a create request with a status value that is not exactly "active", "cancelled", or "completed" (for example "pending" or case variants such as "Active")
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request and does not create an Order

requirements.title: Order representation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL represent an Order with exactly the fields id, status, and timestamp, and SHALL NOT persist additional fields supplied by clients.

acceptance.given: any persisted Order
acceptance.outcome: it contains exactly the fields id, status, and timestamp

acceptance.given: an Order has been created
acceptance.outcome: no extra computed or metadata fields are present beyond id, status, and timestamp

requirements.title: Order in-memory persistence
requirements.statement: The system SHALL persist Order data solely in memory for the lifetime of the service process.

acceptance.given: an Order has been persisted
acceptance.when: the running process serves subsequent read, list, and delete requests
acceptance.outcome: the Order record remains available within the same process instance

acceptance.given: an Order has been persisted
acceptance.outcome: the previously persisted Order records are no longer present

requirements.title: Thread-safe store access
requirements.statement: The system SHALL ensure thread-safe concurrent access to the in-memory store for Order read and write operations.

acceptance.given: multiple concurrent create requests for Orders
acceptance.outcome: each Order is stored exactly once with a unique id and without data corruption

acceptance.given: concurrent read or list requests while Orders are being created or deleted
acceptance.outcome: the responses are internally consistent and do not observe torn or partially updated records

acceptance.given: concurrent delete requests for the same Order UUID
acceptance.outcome: the Order is removed from the store and the store remains consistent

rules.rule: A User id is immutable and is system-assigned as a UUID at creation time.
rules.rationale: Guarantees unique identity without requiring client coordination and prevents identity spoofing or alteration.

rules.rule: The email address acts as a unique key across the entire user population.
rules.rationale: Prevents duplicate accounts and ambiguous identity lookups.

rules.rule: Replacement of a User is a full-record operation requiring id, name, and email; partial updates are not permitted.
rules.rationale: Avoids partial-update ambiguity and keeps the state transfer contract explicit.

rules.rule: A User entity has exactly the fields id, name, and email.
rules.rationale: Keeps the domain model minimal and predictable for clients.

rules.rule: User data persists only in memory for the lifetime of the process; no durable storage or cross-process replication is provided.
rules.rationale: Matches the explicit in-memory store requirement and sets clear durability expectations.

rules.rule: An Order id is immutable and is system-assigned as a UUID at creation time.
rules.rationale: Guarantees unique identity without requiring clients to manage identifier allocation.

rules.rule: The status field of an Order is restricted to the values "active", "cancelled", or "completed".
rules.rationale: Enforces the validated business vocabulary agreed upon for Order status.

rules.rule: An Order entity has exactly the fields id, status, and timestamp.
rules.rationale: Establishes the minimal, agreed-upon schema for the entity.

rules.rule: Order data persists only in memory for the lifetime of the process; no durable storage or cross-process replication is provided.
rules.rationale: Matches the explicit in-memory store requirement and sets clear durability expectations.

rules.rule: All read and write operations against the shared in-memory store are thread-safe.
rules.rationale: Parallel HTTP requests may access the store simultaneously, and race conditions must be prevented.
